behavior_summary: This script implements a guardsman NPC with three gameplay behaviors: a 'U8P_GUARD_EW::look' inspection response, proximity reactions when the player enters its fast-area, and a multi-branch conversation when the player 'uses' (talks to) the guard.
U8P_GUARD_EW::look (U8P_GUARD_EW::look): when inspected, checks Npc::isDead(this). If dead, shows a 'dead guardsman' bark; otherwise shows a 'guardsman' bark. The process then suspends.

U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea (U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea): triggered when an actor enters the guard's proximity. If the guard is alive it runs a short reaction sequence by invoking engine helpers (func091C, func15E7) with suspends between calls — likely to face the player, animate, or update meeting state.

U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule (U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule): writes local U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule/timing values, calls a scheduler/movement helper (func143A) and waits. If process_result() returns true it clears the guard's dead state (Npc::clrDead) and calls func133F — inferred to re-enable or finalize NPC reactivation after a scheduled event.

U8P_GUARD_EW::use (U8P_GUARD_EW::use): handles conversation. It first aggregates several world/player flags to possibly trigger an immediate urgent 'Titan' warning and exit. If a special interrogation condition is met (benticCacked == 1, interrogEW == 0, devonInRule == 0) it pathfinds the guard into position, calls func15E7, sets interrogEW = 1, and enters an interrogation dialog branch. Otherwise it selects a greeting variant depending on guardEWMet, builds a menu (local2) of questions, loops with Ask -> SuspendAssign (local1) -> strcmp-based branching, dynamically modifies the menu (MenuRemove/MenuAdd) and eventually exits on 'Bye'.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD_EW::look: simple two-way branch on Npc::isDead(this) → two barks → suspend.
U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea: check Npc::isDead(this); alive → METHOD::func091C → suspend → METHOD::func15E7 → suspend.
U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule: write several local scheduling flags → METHOD::func143A → suspend → if (process_result()) { Npc::clrDead(this); METHOD::func133F; suspend }.

U8P_GUARD_EW::use: initial aggregation of hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree into local4; if threshold/hasHeart triggers then emit urgent Titan warnings and end. Else if benticCacked==1 && interrogEW==0 && devonInRule==0 then start Npc::pathfind(...) (suspend) → METHOD::func15E7 → set interrogEW=1 → interrogation menu. Otherwise choose greeting based on guardEWMet. Enter a menu loop: Ask(local2) → suspend → SuspendAssign(local1) → strcmp(local1, ...) branching → dynamic MenuRemove/MenuAdd/MenuSet → loop until 'Bye.'
open_questions: Exact semantics of METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F: they appear to be engine/AI helpers for facing/meeting-state updates, scheduling/movement registration, and finalization of reactivation, but their precise effects are not explicit in the excerpt.
Precise threshold logic used to decide the urgent 'Titan' warning (how local4 is compared to the threshold and whether individual flags like hasHeart cause immediate branching).

The in-engine meaning of the numeric U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule/local constants set in U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule (local3=0x05, local2=0x159F, local1=0x583F, local0=0x30) — whether they map to timers, waypoints, or U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule IDs.
Whether setting interrogEW is global across multiple guards or local to this guard instance (flag is global in metadata but the broader scope is not fully shown).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect/description response for the guard NPC. Distinguishes dead vs alive to select a short bark.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_GUARD_EW::look' or inspect action on this NPC (standard U8P_GUARD_EW::look handler).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_GUARD_EW::look handler that emits a short bark for dead vs alive guard

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea
function_map.role: proximity/fast-area trigger reaction; calls engine helpers to perform immediate NPC reaction and meeting-state updates

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule
function_map.role: U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule/timer setup and post-U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule reactivation logic (may clear dead state and resume NPC)

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::use
function_map.role: conversation/dialogue entry; urgent warnings, special interrogation path, greeting variants and a menu-driven dialog loop with dynamic menu manipulation

function_map.name: METHOD::func091C
function_map.role: engine helper invoked on proximity enter (likely face/animation/short reaction)

function_map.name: METHOD::func15E7
function_map.role: common helper invoked to refresh meeting/interaction state or similar bookkeeping

function_map.name: METHOD::func143A
function_map.role: scheduling/movement registration helper called after U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule flags are set

function_map.name: METHOD::func133F
function_map.role: finalization helper invoked after successful scheduled event/process_result()

function_map.name: Npc::pathfind
function_map.role: engine movement/pathfinding process used to move the NPC into position before dialogue

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: clears the NPC dead state (resurrect or mark alive); invoked in U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule reactivation branch

external_calls.purpose: Trigger an immediate NPC reaction (face/animation) when player enters proximity; suspend follows to yield control.
Update NPC meeting/interaction state after the immediate reaction; used across contexts so likely refreshes 'met' bookkeeping.
Register or start scheduling/movement behavior using earlier-written local U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule values.
Clear the NPC's dead state when a scheduled/process_result() condition succeeds (reactivation/resurrection).
Finalize or resume NPC behavior following successful U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule/process completion.
Asynchronously move the guard into position before entering the interrogation branch of dialogue.
Refresh NPC interaction/meeting state after pathfind, prior to continuing the interrogation dialog.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a local counter used to decide whether to emit urgent Titan warning and end conversation

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same urgent-warning counter and can directly trigger an alternate urgent warning branch

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the urgent-warning counter used at conversation start

reads.name: benticCacked
reads.likely_effect: Gates the special Bentic interrogation branch when true (used with interrogEW and devonInRule checks)

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Part of the condition preventing/releasing the interrogation branch (must be 0 to allow interrogation)

reads.name: guardEWMet
reads.likely_effect: Selects greeting variant in conversation (different lines for met vs stranger)

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Used within conversation branches to alter scolding/accusation lines about Bentic

writes.name: interrogEW
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when entering the special Bentic interrogation branch; marks interrogation progress/state so branch isn't re-entered immediately

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a guardsman NPC with three gameplay behaviors: a 'U8P_GUARD_EW::look' inspection response, proximity reactions when the player enters its fast-area, and a multi-branch conversation when the player 'uses' (talks to) the guard.
